description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

surface_forms: on the border of South Shore
description: community in Chicago
description_wiki: South Shore is one of the 77 community areas of Chicago in Illinois, United States.

description: college in Chicago
description_wiki: Wilbur Wright College is a public municipal community college in the Dunning community area in the northwestern side of Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Dauphin County, Pennsylvania, United States and Pennsylvania federated state capital city
description_wiki: Harrisburg ( HARR-iss-burg; Pennsylvania German: Harrisbarrig) is the capital city of the U.S. commonwealth of Pennsylvania.

description: air warfare branch of the U.S. Army from 1926 to 1947
description_wiki: The United States Army Air Corps (USAAC) was the aerial warfare service component of the United States Army between 1926 and 1941.

description: former military base in Orange County, California
description_wiki: Santa Ana Army Air Base (SAAAB) was a World War II-era air base located near Santa Ana, California.

description: city in California, United States
description_wiki: Monterey (  MON-tə-RAY; Spanish: Monterrey) is a city on the southern edge of Monterey Bay, on the Central Coast of California.

description: city in Florida, United States
description_wiki: Carrabelle is a city in Franklin County along Florida's Panhandle, United States.

description: county seat of Leon County, Florida, USA and Florida federated state capital city
description_wiki: Tallahassee ( TAL-ə-HASS-ee) is the capital city of the U.S. state of Florida.

description: light aircraft family
description_wiki: The Piper J-3 Cub is an American light aircraft that was built between 1938 and 1947 by Piper Aircraft.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: commune in Seine-Maritime, France
description_wiki: Le Havre is a major port city in the Seine-Maritime department in the Normandy region of northern France.

description: geographical and cultural region which extends over a part of France and the British Channel Islands
description_wiki: Normandy is a geographical and cultural region in northwestern Europe, roughly coextensive with the historical Duchy of Normandy.

description: 1944 landing operations of the Allied invasion of Normandy
description_wiki: The Normandy landings were the landing operations and associated airborne operations on 6 June 1944 of the Allied invasion of Normandy in Operation Overlord during the Second World War.

description: capital and most populous city in France
description_wiki: Paris is the capital and largest city of France, with an estimated city population of 2.04 million in an area of 105.4 km2 (40.7 sq mi), and a metropolitan population of 13.2 million as of January 2026.

description: river in Western Europe to the North Sea
description_wiki: The Rhine ( RYNE) is one of the major rivers of Europe.

description: country in western Europe
description_wiki: Belgium, officially the Kingdom of Belgium, is a country in Northwestern Europe.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: country in Western Europe
description_wiki: Luxembourg,  officially the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg, is a landlocked country in Western Europe.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

entity: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
surface_forms: the United States dropped the two nuclear bombs
description: 1945 use of nuclear weapons against Japan in World War II
description_wiki: On 6 and 9 August 1945, the United States detonated two atomic bombs over the Japanese cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, respectively, during the final days of World War II. The aerial bombings killed 150,000 to 246,000 people, most of whom were civilians, and remain the first and only uses of nuclear weapons in an armed conflict.

description: federated state, capital and largest city of Germany
description_wiki: Berlin is the capital of Germany, as well as its largest city by both area and population.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: United States general (1897–1978)
description_wiki: Lucius Dubignon Clay (23 April 1898 – 16 April 1978) was a senior officer of the United States Army who was known for his administration of occupied Germany after World War II. He served as the deputy to General of the Army Dwight D. Eisenhower in 1945; deputy military governor, Germany, in 1946; Commander in Chief, United States Forces in Europe and military governor of the United States Zone, Germany, from 1947 to 1949.

description: capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom
description_wiki: London is the capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom, with a population of 9.1 million people in 2024.

description: street circuit at the Berlin Tempelhof Airport, Berlin, Germany
Romani-Belgian /French jazz musician (1910–1953)
description_wiki: Jean Reinhardt (23 January 1910 – 16 May 1953), known by his Romani nickname Django (French: [dʒãŋɡo ʁɛjnaʁt] or [dʒɑ̃ɡo ʁenɑʁt]), was a Belgian-born Romani jazz guitarist and composer who lived most of his life in France.

description: Matson Lines ocean liner
description_wiki: SS Mariposa was an ocean liner launched in 1931, one of four ships in the Matson Lines "White Fleet", which included SS Monterey, SS Malolo, and SS Lurline.
